title: National Register of Historic Places listings in Big Stone County, Minnesota
rows: Monumental Victorian courthouse designed by Fremont D. Orff and completed in 1902 ; Big Stone County 's long-serving seat of government and largest Victorian building
Chicago , Milwaukee , St. Paul and Pacific Depot
Main and Center Sts . 45°27′35″N 96°26′14″W / 45.459722°N 96.437222°W / 45.459722 ; -96.437222 ( Chicago , Milwaukee , St. Paul and Pacific Depot )
Well-preserved example of a small combination depot , built c. 1885 . Also noted as Clinton 's oldest building and a symbol of the impact of railroads on the town . Now houses a city history museum
County Road 25 ( original address ) Current coordinates are 45°17′48″N 96°25′54″W / 45.296763°N 96.431751°W / 45.296763 ; -96.431751 ( District No . 13 School )

Unique octagonal schoolhouse built in 1898 . Also significant as one of the county 's first schools and the first building by a founder of the regionally notable Carlson-Hasslen Construction Company . Moved to the Big Stone County Historical Museum
Well-preserved example of an early Minnesota highway wayside , built 1940-41 by the Works Progress Administration . Also noted for its NPS Rustic landscape architecture by Arthur R. Nichols
Odessa 's oldest public building , built in 1913 ; a rare surviving example of the freestanding brick jailhouses once common in small Minnesota towns
2nd St. , Madison and Monroe Aves. , between Jefferson and Jackson Aves . 45°18′22″N 96°26′47″W / 45.30604°N 96.446314°W / 45.30604 ; -96.446314 ( Ortonville Commercial Historic District )

Long-serving commercial district noted for its uniform Victorian buildings , representative of the region 's small towns but unique for their purple granite trim . Contains 19 contributing properties built 1879-1922

One of west-central Minnesota 's few examples of a Carnegie library and of Mission Revival architecture , built in 1915 . Also significant as a work of St. Paul architect Franklin Ellerbe and the Carlson-Hasslen Construction Company
St. Pauli Norwegian Evangelical Lutheran Church
33022 U.S. Route 75 45°25′35″N 96°25′52″W / 45.426509°N 96.431202°W / 45.426509 ; -96.431202 ( St. Pauli Norwegian Evangelical Lutheran Church )
1896 church - expanded in 1911 - and adjoining cemetery , anchor of a rural Norwegian immigrant community

description: This table lists properties in Big Stone County, Minnesota, that are included in the National Register of Historic Places. It provides details such as the name of the property, the date it was listed, its location, the associated city or town, and a brief description of its historical significance.
